title: Supply Chain Executive (Shipbuilding Projects)
description:
Responsibilities

  • Procurement Planning & Coordination
  • Work closely with the Newbuilding Commercial Team to support procurement planning and coordination for assigned shipbuilding projects.
  • Establish procurement schedules aligned with project timelines and shipyard requirements.
  • Liaise with internal stakeholders (engineering, project management, and finance) to ensure specifications, budgets, and delivery needs are met.

Sourcing & Purchasing

  • Identify and evaluate suppliers for ship equipment, systems, and parts.
  • Obtain, review, and negotiate quotations to achieve competitive pricing and favorable commercial terms.
  • Manage purchase order (PO) creation, approval, and issuance processes.
  • Ensure compliance with company policies and contractual obligations in all procurement transactions.

Contract & Commercial Management

  • Review and negotiate contract terms, ensuring alignment with project and company requirements.
  • Be well-versed in intercompany (Interco) freight terms, incoterms, and logistics coordination.
  • Support commercial evaluations and vendor selection in collaboration with relevant departments.

Tracking, Documentation & Reporting

  • Maintain accurate and up-to-date procurement logs, PO tracking sheets, and supplier databases.
  • Monitor delivery schedules, proactively resolving potential delays or discrepancies.
  • Coordinate with finance and shipyard commercial teams for payment processing, invoice verification, and documentation control.
  • Provide regular procurement status reports and updates to management.

Quality, Compliance & Continuous Improvement

  • Ensure all procurement activities adhere to company standards, shipyard requirements, and classification society rules.
  • Support audits and internal reviews as needed.
  • Recommend improvements to procurement workflows, vendor performance, and cost optimization.

Requirements

  • Diploma/Degree in Supply Chain, Marine Engineering, Business, or related discipline.
  • Minimum 5-8 years of experience in marine or shipbuilding procurement.
  • Strong commercial acumen and negotiation skills.
  • Good understanding of ship equipment, systems, and vendor networks
  • Familiar with incoterms and international freight arrangement.
  • Detail-oriented, organized, and able to handle multiple project timelines.
  • Strong communication and coordination skills.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office and procurement management systems (e.g. SAP, Oracle).


How to Apply:

Interested applicants, kindly click on “APPLY NOW”

We regret only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Important Note: Trust Recruit Pte Ltd is committed to safeguarding your personal data in accordance with the Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA).
